---
title: "WaveDrom"
---

WaveDrom is the de-facto standard for digital timing diagrams in
hardware documentation: signals, clocks, and buses described as JSON,
with per-cycle wave strings that read almost like the waveform itself.
The same engine draws register and bit-field layouts. Below: a
valid/ready handshake with an annotated transfer window (@fig-hs) and a
two-lane instruction encoding (@fig-reg). Click to zoom.

![Valid/ready handshake](figures/handshake.wavedrom.json){#fig-hs}

[view source: `handshake.wavedrom.json`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/handshake.wavedrom.json){.source-link}

![Instruction encoding](figures/register.wavedrom.json){#fig-reg}

[view source: `register.wavedrom.json`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/register.wavedrom.json){.source-link}
